digest.component_or_area: reducer identity keyring + epoch-scoped authorship verification
propositions: Add KEY_ADD/KEY_REVOKE lifecycle events folded by the reducer into an era-scoped keyring, deriving currently-valid keys for the latest epoch.
A post-genesis KEY_ADD/KEY_REVOKE is refused unless accompanied by a valid authorship signature from a currently-valid key of that identity; the genesis key-add is accepted TOFU without a signature.
Key validity is scoped to a monotonic keyring_epoch derived from canonical fold order, never from attacker-controllable event data timestamps.
verify_authorship_at_epoch builds a trust root from only keys whose [added_epoch, revoked_epoch) window contains the epoch, so a key revoked before an event's epoch fails verification.

Epoch counting and keyring must be snapshot-safe: process_snapshot freezes/restores keyring and keyring_epoch so compacted logs reduce to the same keyring and post-snapshot KEY events get contiguous epochs.
New KEY event types must be registered in both the reducer known-type set and the event_append EVENT_TYPES frozenset, with fsck orphan-type coverage and cache version bump.
